tagline: A parasite-host symbiosis economy where alien organisms, body possession, and human emotional stakes create a survival negotiation framework.
visualizationReason: Parasyte lacks a unified system thesis — the parasite-host relationship, Migi-based assimilation, and emotional stakes form a character-driven thriller without a dominant counter/relational/causal structure; standard-cards provides clean functional delivery.

headerFlavor.loreQuote: We are neither allies nor enemies. We are simply... cohabiting.
headerFlavor.sysWarning: // [SYS_OP]: PARASYTE BIOLOGY NETWORK ACTIVE — PARASITE CLASSIFICATION ONLINE

introductionSummary: Parasyte operates as a parasite-host symbiosis economy: alien parasites that take over human bodies by infiltrating the brain, creating a negotiation dynamic between the parasite's survival instinct and the host's consciousness. The story follows Shinichi Izumi, whose parasite Migi takes over his right hand instead of his brain, creating a cooperative hybrid that investigates other parasites who kill humans.

aiInsights.casual: Parasyte is about an alien parasite taking over a teenager's right hand instead of his brain because the parasite arrived too late, and they have to learn to cooperate to survive against other parasites that have killed humans.
aiInsights.deep: The Parasyte universe is a body-possession thriller that explores identity through the parasite-host relationship. Migi and Shinichi negotiate constantly about survival priorities — Migi prioritizes parasite survival, Shinichi prioritizes human emotional connections. The parasites represent pure biological optimization without ethical constraints.

powerSystem.name: Parasite Body Possession
powerSystem.subtitle: Biological Host Takeover
powerSystem.loreDesc: Alien parasites take over human bodies by infiltrating the brain, replacing the human consciousness with the parasite's survival-oriented operating system.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A consciousness-replacement operation where the parasite overwrites the host's cognitive architecture with its own survival-optimized processing.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Migi taking over Shinichi's right hand instead of his brain because it arrived late — a timing error that created a cooperative rather than adversarial relationship.

powerSystem.name: Migi Intelligence
powerSystem.subtitle: Parasite Cognitive Architecture
powerSystem.loreDesc: Migi develops human-like intelligence over time, eventually achieving equal cognitive capacity to humans through adaptation.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A learning-capable biological node that optimizes its cognitive processing based on human interaction data.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Migi developing ethics and emotional attachment through exposure to human culture and Shinichi's relationships.

powerSystem.name: Human Emotional Stakes
powerSystem.subtitle: Host Consciousness Preservation
powerSystem.loreDesc: Shinichi's human consciousness is preserved even with Migi sharing his body — creating constant negotiation about priorities and survival ethics.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A dual-consciousness negotiation framework where the host's emotional priorities compete with the parasite's biological optimization.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Shinichi refusing to kill Gotou despite Migi's recommendations — choosing human ethics over parasite survival logic.

rankings.topTierLore: The Type-Talker parasites are the highest intelligence class, capable of language, strategy, and complex social manipulation.
rankings.topTierSystem: Maximum intelligence parasites that have fully adapted to human society and can operate with human-level strategic planning.

tiers.name: Parasite (Human-Form)
tiers.loreDesc: Parasites that have taken human bodies and operate in society, maintaining a human facade.
tiers.systemDesc: Infiltrator nodes with full human social access.

tiers.name: Parasite (Basic)
tiers.loreDesc: Lower-intelligence parasites that retain their original alien forms and are easily identifiable.
tiers.systemDesc: Low-capability biological nodes operating without social integration.

factions.name: Migi / Shinichi Hybrid
factions.loreDesc: The protagonist's cooperative parasite-host relationship — the only known case of successful cohabitation rather than brain takeover.
factions.systemDesc: A dual-consciousness node where both host and parasite have equal decision-making authority, creating unique negotiation dynamics.

factions.name: Human Hunters (Parasites)
factions.loreDesc: Parasites who target humans as food sources — they kill humans to survive, creating a predator-prey conflict with Shinichi.
factions.systemDesc: Adversarial biological nodes optimizing for human consumption without ethical constraints.

factions.name: Hamelin Organization
factions.loreDesc: The organization that controls parasites and uses them for targeted assassinations — the institutional threat behind individual parasite encounters.
factions.systemDesc: An institutional network that has weaponized parasite biology for political assassinations.

rules.name: Parasites Must Consume Humans to Reproduce
rules.loreDesc: The alien parasites require human flesh to reproduce — every parasite is an existential threat to human survival by definition.
rules.loreConsequence: There is no negotiated peace possible with parasites who need to consume humans — the conflict is biological, not ideological.
rules.systemEquivalent: A zero-sum survival constraint: parasite prosperity requires human destruction, making coexistence biologically impossible.

rules.name: Brain Takeover is Irreversible
rules.loreDesc: Once a parasite fully infiltrates the brain, the original human consciousness is permanently overwritten — there is no cure or reversal.
rules.loreConsequence: Any victim of full brain takeover is permanently lost — the human they were no longer exists.
rules.systemEquivalent: A one-way consciousness replacement with no rollback capability — the original node is permanently overwritten.

rules.name: Migi Cannot Take Over Shinichi's Brain
rules.loreDesc: Migi arrived too late and only controlled the right hand — this timing error created the only non-adversarial parasite-host relationship.
rules.loreConsequence: The entire premise of the series depends on a timing accident — if Migi had arrived first, Shinichi would be gone.
rules.systemEquivalent: A critical-path dependency on a random timing event — the cooperative relationship exists because of a scheduling error.

characters.name: Shinichi Izumi
characters.title: Parasite Host / Protagonist
characters.loreBio: A teenager whose right hand was taken over by Migi when the parasite arrived too late to reach his brain — now they share one body in an uneasy cooperative relationship.
characters.systemBio: A dual-consciousness hybrid node where the host (Shinichi) provides emotional intelligence and social context while Migi provides parasite-level combat capabilities and biological optimization.
characters.signatureMoment: Refusing to kill Gotou despite having the power to do so — choosing human ethics over parasite survival logic, demonstrating his human consciousness hasn't been overridden.
characters.keyAbility: Migi Combat Integration / Human Ethics / Emotional Intelligence

characters.name: Migi
characters.title: Type-Talker Parasite
characters.loreBio: The parasite who accidentally took over Shinichi's right hand instead of his brain — over time he develops human-like intelligence and genuine emotional attachment to Shinichi.
characters.systemBio: A biological optimization node that started with pure survival logic but gradually developed ethical considerations through exposure to human emotional intelligence.
characters.signatureMoment: Declaring he would kill himself if Shinichi dies — the first time a parasite showed genuine emotional attachment to a human.
characters.keyAbility: Migi Form / Parasite Combat / Biological Optimization

characters.name: Gotou (Migi's Type)
characters.title: Parasite Leader
characters.loreBio: The most intelligent parasite, a former human whose brain was taken over and who now leads the parasite community, pursuing a 'peaceful coexistence' that requires human extinction.
characters.systemBio: A post-human intelligence node that retained human strategic thinking but lost human ethics — his plan for parasite-human coexistence requires eliminating humanity.
characters.signatureMoment: Revealing his plan to use parasite-produced food to replace human agriculture, eliminating the need to kill humans — a solution that would make humanity economically irrelevant rather than directly killed.
characters.keyAbility: Parasite Physiology / Strategic Planning / Human Mimicry

characters.name: Kana (Migi's love interest)
characters.title: Human / Journalist
characters.loreBio: A human girl who is partially possessed by a parasite — creating an unstable hybrid state where she's caught between human emotions and parasite drives.
characters.systemBio: A compromised node whose consciousness is partially overwritten, creating unpredictable decision-making that serves neither human nor parasite interests cleanly.
characters.signatureMoment: Being partially possessed by a parasite and struggling to maintain her human identity while fighting her parasite impulses.
characters.keyAbility: Partial Parasite Control / Journalist Investigation / Human Emotions

relationships: An accidental symbiosis that develops into genuine mutual respect — Shinichi provides human ethics and emotional intelligence, Migi provides combat capabilities and parasite biology.
A cooperative dual-consciousness node where both components have equal decision authority and have developed genuine mutual investment in each other's survival.
Shinichi's fundamental disagreement with Gotou's solution: Gotou wants parasite dominance over humans, Shinichi wants coexistence without human extinction.
A terminal conflict between two optimization targets: parasite prosperity (Gotou) vs. human survival (Shinichi).
Migi develops genuine emotional attachment to Kana through Shinichi's exposure — the first time a parasite shows human-like emotional investment.
A capability-architecture evolution: Migi developing emotional processing beyond pure biological optimization.

counterplay: Parasites have superior physical capabilities but lack human creativity and emotional intelligence — humans can outthink parasites strategically even if they can't outfight them physically.
The police and human characters use strategic planning and group coordination to hunt parasites despite being physically outmatched.
A strategic-over-physical optimization advantage: human intelligence networks can coordinate to counter physically superior biological nodes.
Migi can analyze and counter other parasites' biological attack patterns using his own parasite physiology — he's essentially a built-in defense system.
Migi's parasite combat capabilities allow him to engage other parasites at their own physical level, making Shinichi effectively parasite-immune.
A biological counter-platform: Migi's parasite capabilities provide real-time counter-analysis of other parasite attack vectors.

causalEvents: Migi arrives at Shinichi's house but is too late to reach his brain before the body falls asleep
Migi takes over the right hand instead of overwriting Shinichi's consciousness — the only successful non-adversarial parasite-host relationship
A random timing event created the only cooperative parasite-host relationship: if Migi had arrived 30 seconds earlier or later, Shinichi would be gone.
A critical-path dependency on a scheduling error: the entire series premise depends on a random biological timing mismatch.
Gotou reveals his plan for parasite-produced food to replace human agriculture
A solution that would make humanity economically irrelevant rather than requiring their direct killing — a 'peaceful' human extinction through market displacement
Gotou's solution is terrifying because it's rational: replacing human agriculture with more efficient parasite-produced food would eliminate the need to kill humans while achieving the same outcome.
A post-human optimization that treats humanity as an inefficiency rather than an enemy: economic displacement instead of direct conflict.

anomalies.loreDesc: Migi and Shinichi's cooperative relationship is the only known case of successful partial parasite-host cohabitation — every other human taken over by a parasite is permanently lost.
anomalies.systemDesc: A zero-rate success in an otherwise 100% failure system: the cooperative outcome exists only because of the specific timing of Migi's arrival.
anomalies.impact: Shinichi and Migi represent a unique case that could theoretically provide insights into parasite control, but no other human has survived parasite contact.
anomalies.ruleViolated: Brain takeover is irreversible — except in this one case

systemQuestions: Why doesn't Migi just take over Shinichi's brain when he's sleeping?
By the time Migi could theoretically do this, he has developed emotional investment in Shinichi's survival and human relationships — the parasite developed ethics through exposure to human culture.
What makes Gotou's plan more terrifying than direct conflict?
Gotou's plan achieves the same outcome (human extinction) through economic efficiency rather than violence — making it seem rational and difficult to oppose on moral grounds.
What is the fundamental conflict in Parasyte?

The fundamental conflict is biological optimization without ethics: parasites operate on pure survival logic while humans have emotional constraints that limit their optimization. Gotou represents the nightmare of pure biological optimization with human-level intelligence.

hero: Neither ally nor enemy — we simply share the same body.

The parasite-host relationship in Parasyte is a negotiation between pure biological optimization (Migi) and human emotional ethics (Shinichi) — the only way they coexist is through mutual adaptation and respect.
